programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.sub-title: South West and North East London, Revisited
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with househunting couples in London. Sarah and Ian and Louise and Matt were two couples looking to buy their first homes together, but both couples needed to compromise on where to buy. Sarah and Ian had to looked beyond their beloved Balham, while Louise and Matt had second thoughts about settling in north east London.

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: Stacey helps Jo and Darryl, whose seaside dream in Devon has turned into a nightmare. Can Stacey turn the tide and help them realise their dream of an upside-down living space?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Manchester, professional crooner Danny takes his guests on a culinary journey from Spain to Thailand. Will Dannys night be a success, or nothing to write home about? And who will win the £1000 prize?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On payment day, a number of BandBers are sure that their competitors have been doing some tit-for-tat marking. When the dust settles, wholl win the week?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Italy, can Donna keep up as holiday reservations fly in? In France, will unseasonable weather put a dampener on Paul and Melissas big summer event?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Jan and her son Jordan find a holiday home near the thriving town of La Duquesa. Are any of Lauras options perfect enough for Jan to put in an offer?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Jean Johansson is back in the countryside near Tomar in central Portugal with Alison and Mike, who she helped find their dream home. And Ben Hillman catches up with Whitney and Rose to see the changes theyve made in Punta Prima.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Southend-on-Sea, the first to host is dog-fancier executive PA Julie-Ann Rayner. Julie-Anns a dinner party veteran and has high hopes that her `taste of the Sea menu - with not one, but two fish courses - will have her guests hooked, assuming that they all like fish, that is. If her fishy feast doesnt go down a treat, Julie-Ann has some musical mayhem up her sleeve.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its grandmother Carols turn to host in Southend-on-Sea. Shes pretty confident in the kitchen, and her menu includes scallops and roast lamb.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its night three of the competition in Southend, where science teacher and self confessed geek Damian Loneragan is hosting.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the penultimate night of the competition in Southend and all eyes are on barmaid and self confessed airhead Steph Barrett as she hosts a dinner party in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize. The day gets off to a disastrous start as Steph attempts to make pastry for the very first time with hilarious results. Things get back on track after an emergency visit from her Nan who brings round some ready-made pastry.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the last night of the competition and the turn of DJ Scott Langley to host a dinner party in the hope of winning the £1,000 prize. Scotts a pretty good cook and is soon flying ahead with his cooking, but hes taken a massive gamble.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Graham look for their dream holiday home in Mar Menor, Europes largest coastal lagoon, on Spains Costa Blanca. Jasmine Harman shows them five impressive properties for their £70k.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with two couples who wanted to buy close to the English Riviera in Torquay. Newlyweds Sam and Jamie wanted to put a difficult couple of years behind them, while Geoff and Sharon were searching for a large family home.

programme.title: George Clarkes Building Home
programme.desc: Keith and Kate return to the Scottish seaside town of Dunoon, where Kate has fond memories and family ties. But making a Victorian house work as a modern home proves challenging.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil head to Nottingham, where Lexie and Ed are at a stalemate. Their four-bed, one-bathroom home is fit to burst, and Ed just wants to pack up and move to a bigger house. But Lexie thinks, with Kirsties help, that the house could work.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: At Queens Medical Centre in Nottingham, staff help a 16-year-old with a serious illness. Steve is rushed into Resus after falling from his motorbike. And 63-year-old Paul has come to AandE after partially amputating his thumb at work.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A new series at Queens, Nottingham. Ronalds has sliced through his ankle at work. Owens put his shoulder out. And Jeremys crash on a bike leads to a shocking discovery.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett explores the impact of the Normans on southern Europe and the Middle East. The Normans spread south in the 11th century, winning control of southern Italy and the island of Sicily. There they created their most prosperous kingdom, where Christianity and Islam co-existed in relative harmony and mutual tolerance, but events in the Middle East provoked the more aggressive side of the Norman character.

programme.title: Legends
programme.sub-title: Neil Sedaka: Thats When the Music Takes Me
programme.desc: Documentary profiling songwriter and performer Neil Sedaka, the man behind huge hits like `Oh Carol, Is This the Way to Amarillo and Solitaire. After recording teenage anthems in the early 1960s, he turned to writing songs for artists such as the Monkees, Tom Jones and Tony Christie. A move to England in the early 1970s was the launch pad for a remarkable performing comeback.

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: From asteroids and bursts of radiation to microscopic alien viruses, each one of these cosmic dangers may end human civilization as it is known.

programme.sub-title: The Life and Death of the Sun

programme.desc: At a mysterious site in the Gobi Desert, experts discover a monster graveyard; the groundbreaking find contains the treasures of the alien beasts that once lived in this strange world.

programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Stolen Nazi Treasure

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Its one of the most famous stories from the Bible: God sends a flood to wipe out nearly every living thing on Earth, except for Noah, his family and the animals he saves in his fabled ark.

programme.desc: For millennia, plagues, curses, droughts, and the violent destruction of cities have been blamed on the wrath of angry gods. But were these divine punishments mere myth, or is there proof they are based on real events?

programme.desc: Ancient practices such as prayer, blood sports, voodoo and exorcism are often thought to be relics of bygone superstition, but there is evidence they have an impact on the physical world.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouses attempts to boost Penfolds confidence backfires when it leads to a ferocious games monster escaping into the real world.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When the family visits a farm, Cheese bonds with a dairy cow and brings it home to live with them, which makes her siblings lives miserable. Cheese must learn to acknowledge their feelings or risk losing her real family and her new bovine one.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings lay hands on an ancient console, and Grizzy is transported into the game.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Call the Cops!: Plankton goes to jail, but the secret formula is taken as evidence! SpongeBob and Mr. Krabs plot to get it back.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigate strange events. When the agents are accidentally transported to the future, they need to figure out what day it is or risk a catastrophe!

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis and the gang cant wait for their all-day PE training day with Grizzly Griller and are baffled when their idol makes them learn a range of boring non-sports that only Walter excels at.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: Joy has hired a new inflatable assault course for the leisure centre, and the boys are tasked with inflating it. Once done, Danny challenges Lance to a race - but who will be victorious?

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: There is a way that Earth could have a second sun, so the team try it out to see what happens.

programme.title: Bitesize: 9-11 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about healthy hearts and the components of blood.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Cam, Tyler and Aaron explore the wonders of the wetlands and meet the wildlife heroes working hard to help protect its animal inhabitants. Its an early start for Tyler as he heads to Devon hoping to bump into a beaver and discover why this furry eco engineer is having a huge impact on the land and its wildlife. Cam is on the outskirts of Manchester to lend a hand restoring a wetland habitat that is helping combat climate change, all thanks to a super-plant called sphagnum moss.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: From Henry VIIIs wine fountain, to Empress Dowagers marble boat, Horrible Histories talks money, jewellery and untold treasures.

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Rickys ready to reveal his latest diamond encrusted arty creation but is shocked to discover its been stolen! Luckily detective Gav puts on his deerstalker and comes to the rescue whilst Ricky gains inspiration from artist Iris Scott and her finger print painting. But can the pals get to the bottom of the mystery of the missing diamonds?

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.sub-title: Real Mutants, Volcanoes and Drinking Vomit
programme.desc: The Real Mutants assemble, people are drinking vomit and electrocuting themselves for science, and the earth and moon have an earworm about the solar system.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads off to the largest state in America, home to some of the most impressive predators on Earth. Out at sea he uses some sonar technology to track down massive humpback whales. Then he braves the freezing coastal waters to get a close up look at the worlds furriest mammal, perfectly adapted for the freezing waters and while cute looking, they are prolific killers with very sharp minds.

programme.desc: The hunt is on for grossest thing ever seen on `Operation Ouch.

programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `van, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. See lots of different vans, a yellow van, a white van, a big van and even a veggie van. Theres a mini van, a flower van, a fast van and a camper van.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu fails to realise that Biba is scared by a game they are playing together.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Fred and Dana start Happy Helpers Day - the best helper gets a pie! Wilf wants the reward and hopes to learn to help others. Can the Piggies teach him?

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a man picking apples in an orchard. He empties the apples into a box, puts the box in his truck and goes off to find a bigger one. While hes gone, the Twirlywoos decide they want to try picking apples themselves.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends take Bonnie the dog for a wash and blow dry at the local dog groomers. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble attempts to dog train the Puppy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies find different uses for their favourite things and a child in Tummy Tales makes a seaside picture out of lots of things from a make-and-do box. In Teletubbyland a large tube appears. The Teletubbies use it as a telescope and as a loud speaker.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Connor, Bonnie, Ian, Dhilan, Addi, Gracie and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyones bags are wooden spoons which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate wooden spoons inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The popular nursery rhyme `Little Miss Muffet features, as does a story involving Baby Bear.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a building site with her new friend Dreya to play a game of I Spy.

programme.title: I Can Grow It

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: A dusty, old box makes Rebecca sneeze! Murphi dreams up some make ideas to help reveal what is inside, including a tissue box monster and a flamingo sock puppet.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas invite Kid Brocc to a sleepover, but can he adjust to a new place?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Not wanting to go to bed, Bluey tries to dream about being a nocturnal fruit bat so she can stay up all night long and soon finds herself flying through the night sky.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: A nice and fun song that is all about the joys and magic of reading.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Ted wants to camp out in the garden, and with sister Sofias help he puts up a tent for them both to sleep in. Dads in charge of the barbecue and cooks up a yummy camping feast of hot dogs and toasted marshmallows. Before they settle down for a night in the great outdoors, Ted and his family talk about some of the nocturnal animals they may hear during the night.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lolas family are from Spain, a country in southern Europe. She is making a traditional Spanish dish called empanadas for her friends, with a fish and vegetable filling and a side of Spanish dancing!

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Benjamin and his brothers want to find out about teeny tiny creatures that crawl - caterpillars. Chantelle meets Ezzie to find out how caterpillars grow and change into cocoons and chrysalis.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Guide dog Kika uses her super sight to help Dr Amit and his friend Alana play a game of sound tennis. Kika tries not to chase the noisy balls at the sports centre.

programme.title: Puffin Rock

programme.title: Small Potatoes

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: As everyone huddles in Sonnys caravan during a storm, Toad admits hes frightened of the sound. Can Yukee find a way to use the pitter-pitter-pitter-patter of the rain to calm him?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the Squirrels meet a lion with unfortunate breath and teach him a special song to help him learn how to brush his teeth.

programme.title: Yakka Dee

We see red beans, green beans, long, thin and lean beans. We see a friend grow some beans, pick them and then eat them for tea.

programme.title: Alphablocks

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Robert is ecstatic when he is recommended for the FBI job, that is, until it becomes apparent that Maries intrusive ways may be his undoing.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: At a party, Ray and Debra do a dead-on impersonation of Frank and Marie that brings down the house, but when Frank and Marie turn the tables and do a Ray and Debra imitation, they dont find it funny at all.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Since she is the only one who knows that Chandler plans to ask Monica to marry him, Phoebe helps Chandler shop for a wedding ring, while Rachels wish for Paul to reveal his emotions creates a new set of problems.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandlers romantic marriage proposal is ruined when Monicas former boyfriend returns, Joey accidentally buys a boat, and a dormitory incident reminds Ross how young his girlfriend is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is left reeling from Richards declaration that he loves her and wants to marry her.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra stumbles across a cassette tape that contains a message from Rays college girlfriend, she wants to know why he has saved it all of these years.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is ready to speak with Ally about the facts of life only to find that Allys interest has changed from where babies come from to the meaning of life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides to run for school president but Ray isnt sure he can handle the twins by himself during all those meetings so he votes against her.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray finally feels comfortable enough to call Debras mother `mom, he wants Debra to reciprocate and start calling Marie `mom, but, when Debra finally gets the words out, she is completely stunned at Maries reaction.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra takes offence when Marie accuses her of being rude and the two wind up fighting and not speaking to each other, but when Ray tries to sort it out he only makes a bad situation worse.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Roberts attempt to play peacemaker backfires, escalating the Cold War between Marie and Debra.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra accuses Ray of still not being able to pick up on her `not in the mood signals, they fondly recall the first night they spent together.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Depressed that Marie and Debra cant seem to resolve their differences, Robert decides to join a support group that the family believes is a cult.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides she and Ray should go to see a marriage counsellor. Although Ray is completely against the idea, he quickly changes his attitude when they meet the therapist, Pamela, who instantly takes a liking to him.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts an unmissable night of entertainment filmed at Londons prestigious Theatre Royal, Drury Lane. Jermaine Jenas bravely hands over his phone in Send to All, pop sensation Ellie Goulding performs her single Like a Saviour, a contestant has the surprise of her life when her episode of The Wheel keeps getting stranger, and theres a breathtaking duet from our two Unexpected Stars of the Show.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is toasting Chandler and Monicas engagement, but after Chandler fails to come up with the goods in the bedroom, he begins to worry about the consequences. Elsewhere, Monica thinks Rachel is trying to steal her glory when she catches her kissing Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monicas dream of a fairytale wedding fizzles out after she learns what her parents have done with her wedding fund. Meanwhile, Rachel fumes when a teasing Joey stumbles across her juicy late-night reading material.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandlers desperate effort to bond with Monicas dad by playing racquetball backfires when he accidentally gets too up-close-and-personal. Elsewhere, Rachel tries to teach `landlubber Joey how to sail without falling overboard, while Monica seeks to replicate Phoebes late grandmothers secret recipe by deconstructing her last remaining cookie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel must hire an assistant and agonises over whether to hire an efficient, experienced woman, or sexy young Tag Jones. Ignoring her friends advice, she hires Tag and soon regrets it. To make matters worse, Tag is attracted to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Engagement Picture
programme.desc: While taking an engagement picture for himself and Monica, Chandler cant stop pulling a strange face, Rachel enlists Joey to take Tag out for a night on the town, and Phoebe and Ross date a couple in the middle of a divorce.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Nap Partners
programme.desc: Wedding fever continues when Monica cant decide whether to appoint Rachel or Phoebe as her maid of honour. To help Monica decide, Joey and Ross test them on how they would react in certain wedding situations.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rosss Library Book
programme.desc: Ross is elated that the university library contains his doctoral dissertation, but he is shocked to discover that it is located in a remote corner frequently used by students having sex. Elsewhere, the tables are turned on Joey when Erin is the one to just want sex and not a relationship.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Doesnt Like Dogs
programme.desc: Joey may have ruined Rachels chances with Tag when he opens his mouth during Thanksgiving dinner, and Phoebe reveals that she has been secretly keeping a cute dog in her room for a few days while its owner is away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Candy
programme.desc: Rachel is dating her assistant, Tag, but they must keep it a secret at work because of the companys anti-dating policy. Her friends warn her that the relationship will only cause her trouble, but just for fun, she becomes more brazen about it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo
programme.desc: Ross unexpectedly finds himself looking after Ben and decides to do something special for Christmas. Since all the Santa costumes have been rented out, Ross dons the best outfit left and entertains Ben as the holiday armadillo.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess wants to be Coachs friend. Nothing has worked so far until she realizes that her only way in is through his obsession with the Pistons.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: When Nick has an awkward run-in with his ex, Caroline, Jessica tries to convince him he can still be friends with her, by introducing him to her former boyfriend, Berkley.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: When Butters gets caught up in an Asian turf war his parents decide medication is the only answer.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: When Stan gets a Tween Wave CD for his birthday his parents ban him from listening to this `cr...

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: With bullying reaching epidemic proportions in America, Stan volunteers to make an anti-bullying video. Will this help Butters deal with his source of terror?

programme.title: South Park
programme.sub-title: I Should Have Never Gone Ziplining
programme.desc: Determined to make the last day of Spring Break count, the boys spend a day ziplining in the Colorado Mountains. Unfortunately, high in the Rockies, far from their families, their trip takes a bad turn. Chances of survival are slim, all they can do is wait to be rescued, but will help come too late?

programme.title: John Mulaney: New in Town

programme.desc: Desiree Burch welcomes comedian Sofie Hagen into her dreamy diner. Together they lament the struggles of plane seatbelt extenders and discuss Sofie hooking up with someone her neighbour.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Birth Mother
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler are forced to pretend to be a reverend and a doctor when their prospective childs birth mother gets the wrong end of the stick. Meanwhile, Joeys date with Phoebes friend doesnt go well, and Ross is left with an inappropriate outfit for a date with a sexy salesgirl.

programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert think he has finally found the one in Angela. Shes an international-trade lawyer whos bright, speaks four languages, clearly loves Robert, and seems too good to be true.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Marie visits an optician and winds up with a pair of glasses just in time for the holidays. Unfortunately, she ends up putting the whole family on edge when she starts seeing them for better and for worse.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Raymond lies and tells Debra that hes already bought her an incredibly thoughtful Christmas present, he frantically tries to figure out what to get her.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank must make amends with his granddaughter, Ally, when she catches him stealing food at the supermarket.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Rays friend, Andy, gets a job booking guests for a local sports radio show, Ray offers to appear as a guest, only to find out that the shows host, Jerry Musso, hates him.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert finally decides to propose to Amy. Robert confides his decision to Raymond, and then convinces Ray to accompany him to meet Amys ultra-conservative parents.

programme.sub-title: The One With All the Cheesecakes
programme.desc: Chandler and Rachel pig out on a neighbours cheesecake that was mistakenly delivered to Chandler. They feel bad, but not quite guilty enough to refrain from eating a second one that has also been wrongly delivered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Theyre Up All Night
programme.desc: During their fist night together, Rachel and Tag realise they forgot to ship out an important package at the office.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes new job as a telemarketer gets off to a tricky start when the first person she calls, an office worker called Earl, tells her he feels unappreciated and wants to kill himself. Phoebe keeps him on the telephone, trying to persuade him otherwise and finally takes the desperate measure of going to his office to convince him to stay alive.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty
programme.desc: Celebrating Rachels 30th birthday inspires the friends to reminisce about their own 30th birthdays. Joey got angry with God for breaking their deal, Ross bought himself a sports car in an attempt to recapture his youth, and Monica got blind drunk and embarrassed herself at her surprise party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joeys New Brain
programme.desc: Joey accidentally informs a cast member that she will be fired so that his character will get her characters brain, and Ross plans a surprise for Monica and Chandlers wedding - he wants to play the bagpipes.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Truth About London
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler fondly reminisce about London until a startling revelation leaves Chandler floored. Meanwhile, Rachel teaches Ben practical jokes to play on Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Cheap Wedding Dress
programme.desc: Monica regrets telling a fellow bride-to-be about a cheaper bridal shop, and later she has to fight the woman tooth and nail for a unique wedding dress before competing with her to hire Chandlers favourite band for the night. Meanwhile, Joey and Ross discover that theyre dating the same girl.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is overjoyed to be nominated for a `Soapie, the third most prestigious award in daytime dramas. Meanwhile, engaged Monica frets when she realises she will never experience the thrill of a new relationship again, Ross spots Chandler sneaking out of a male strip club, and Phoebe meets a cute guy.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross and Monicas Cousin
programme.desc: Monica is so perturbed by Chandlers instant attraction to her gorgeous visiting cousin that she persuades Ross to let her stay at his apartment. Despite being her blood relative, Ross cannot restrain himself from attempting to kiss her. She escapes to Phoebes apartment, but her beauty flabbergasts even Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels Big Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel runs into an old sorority sister with whom she once shared a passionate encounter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: With only four weeks until their wedding, Monica and Chandler try to write their vows. Chandler enlists the boys help, while Monica does the same with the girls.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica convinces a reluctant Chandler to travel to Las Vegas to invite his estranged cross-dressing father to their wedding. Meanwhile, Rachel is furious that she is the only friend who Monica has not allowed to drive the Porsche that her father gave her.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: Skipton is the location today for David Dickinson and his dealers Henry Nicholls, Cheryl Hakeney, Tony Geering and Jo Brayshaw. A carved shell cameo gets the money rolling in at auction.

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is in Liverpools Anglican Cathedral to celebrate the 1.5 million pounds the public have taken in cash since the antiques show began, he recalls some of the best auction results, biggest bargains and the boldest sellers ever to visit the Dealers Den.

programme.title: The Last Woodsmen

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Naturalist builder Cat Taylor builds a remote sanctuary in the piney woods of Northwest Texas; she builds the two-story house using a cob mixture and adds touches of comfort to entertain family members.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Professional tree-house builder Michael "Murph" Murphy wants to build a cottage retreat for his family in Washington; he enlists his friends to create natural siding and a massive fishing net for lounging above the living space.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.sub-title: The One on a Caravan Site
programme.desc: Like any bride, Lauren wants to feel ultra special and unique on her wedding day. However, groom Courtney plans their nuptials at the same caravan park Lauren visits every single year.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Essex couple Jas and Bobby could be heading for rocky waters. One of them wants a quiet wedding, while the other favours a Titanic-style night to remember.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Sophia wants a blingy wedding that will put her centre stage. Love-struck groom Micky hopes to impress with a winter wonderland event and not be left out in the cold with a frosty reception.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Steven J. Cloobeck goes back undercover, this time focusing on properties that Diamond Resorts International acquired from the bankrupted Pacific Monarch Resorts.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Steve Greenbaum is the co-founder and CEO of PostNet, a company that helps small businesses with everything they need to thrive, from printing and delivery to graphic design. Steve goes undercover in his own organisation.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon continues to restore the failing Towns Inn in Harpers Ferry, West Virginia. After witnessing abysmal food hygiene practices in the restaurant, Gordon is left in disbelief as he uncovers terrifying cleanliness issues throughout the hotel.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon checks into the luxurious Lakeview Hotel on Lake Chelan, Washington, where the bedrooms are empty and the hotels restaurant and ice cream parlour are failing.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon visits Newtown in Pennsylvania to redeem the historic Brick Hotel. Owner Verindar runs the establishment like an angry tyrant and the employees have had enough.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon heads to The Beachfront Inn and Inlet, a `Caribbean-style resort hotel in Fort Pierce, Florida. Young and inexperienced owner Brian is making guests time at the hotel miserable with dusty rooms, late-night concerts and poor service.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Loudonville, Ohio, to rescue the struggling Landolls Mohican Castle, a custom-built, multi-million-dollar hotel set in an 1100-acre woodland estate.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, as Jon Richardson and Judi Love take on Rob Beckett and Katherine Parkinson. Spencer Jones is in Dictionary Corner.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Cats Does Countdown does Christmas. Jon Richardson and Daisy May Cooper play Rob Beckett and Judi Love. With Katie Norris and Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: Bill Bailey Tinselworm
programme.desc: Filmed at Wembley Arena at the end of his UK sell-out tour, the show includes Bill Baileys trademark musical inventiveness and verbal brilliance as well as films and animation by award-winning film-maker Joe Magee.

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: When Fran takes piano lessons and Bernard falls in love, Manny significantly contributes to both situations.

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Fran cant sleep in the heat, Bernard needs a girlfriend to stop him staring and Manny cant say what will happen if it gets to 88 degrees- Dave Syndrome.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Compilation episode of the comedy statistics quiz. Jimmy Carr hosts, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and an array of celebrity guests.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Uncut
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr and team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by guests Joe Wilkinson, Ellie Taylor, Natalie Cassidy and Gabby Logan.

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: When Fran takes piano lessons and Bernard falls in love, Manny significantly contributes to both situations.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell and Cameron get more than they bargained for when they hire a Hispanic gardener who cant speak English.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell sows the seed that one of Jays buddies is secretly gay, and Claire wages war against a remote control.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam decides to see the movie Willow instead of spending Mothers Day with Beverly. Barry tires of his status as the familys middle child.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Craigs grand plan for his wedding to Sofia was to combine his two great loves: her and beer. He threw an Oktoberfest-themed do, complete with short leather pants and steins full of lager. The pair relive the moments that led up to their big day.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Cam is finally getting a chance to shine when he is asked to preside over the high school graduation ceremony at the last minute, while Jay gives the commencement address.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The family looks back at how they all celebrated their birthdays over the past year, as the birth of Haley and Dylans twins is approaching.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the first of an explosive two-part special, Gordon tries to turn around the fortunes of Los Angeles restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Its only been open for 16 months but has already changed its menu more than 10 times, as well as going through 10 chefs and 10 waitresses, and owner Alan has spent his sons inheritance on the restaurant without permission, leading to much family conflict.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly marshals everyone for one last family vacation to Disneyland. Erica worries that Geoff wants to break up. Guest starring Christie Brinkley.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam gets a blast from the past in the shape of Dana - his first love. Meanwhile, Erica and Barry are heading to college with nowhere to live.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay reluctantly takes Phil on a model-airplane excursion, and Gloria goes shopping with Alex. Meanwhile, Claire and Manny get to know one another as siblings.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire and Mitchells mother, Dede, pays a surprise visit wanting to make up for the trouble she caused at Jay and Glorias wedding. No one is eager to see her.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homers marriage is tested when they travel to a tropical island to compete on Marges favourite reality competition show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: My Way or the Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: God and St Peter reflect on what merits a soul making its way into heaven, while citizens of Springfield look back on their divine encounters.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Baby You Cant Drive My Car
programme.desc: A self-driving car company brings its fun offices to Springfield and that means big trouble for the power plant.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Kim Kardashian lookalike Becky needs a manly man to love her and her snake. Is leather-clad Lee the dude? And is Sean turn the settling-down kind of toy boy Emma wants?

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The explosive drama continues as Gordon is still in Los Angeles struggling to bring together the fragmented Saffron family and their failing restaurant, the Burger Kitchen. Last time, son Daniel nearly came to blows with the head chef, and Gordon was shocked to learn that dad Alan had taken from Daniels inheritance, without his knowledge, to help fund the start-up of the restaurant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During the Christmas holiday, Marge attempts to muster the wherewithal to kick down-on-his-luck Gil out of the Simpson house.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer trashes a marina, he is forced to pay off his debt to the locals by becoming a fisherman. His sea legs are put to the test when a storm strikes his boat.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay has a particularly hard time in the locker room, Claire becomes upset when she realises that her friend Valerie is better than her and Mitchell assists Gloria with legal issues.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Its Valentines Day and, while Phil and Claire plan a special trip, Jay woos Gloria by taking her to a stand-up show. Expect varying romantic results.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homers marriage is tested when they travel to a tropical island to compete on Marges favourite reality competition show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: My Way or the Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: God and St Peter reflect on what merits a soul making its way into heaven, while citizens of Springfield look back on their divine encounters.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Baby You Cant Drive My Car
programme.desc: A self-driving car company brings its fun offices to Springfield and that means big trouble for the power plant.

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: The coach winds its way to Grândola, and two more newbies hop aboard. A salsa-dancing championship is splendidly spiced up when the group swap partners, and the celebs fight for their tour guides affection on a wild and wonderful treasure hunt.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Maggie wreaks a path of destruction in the Simpson home after Marge follows advice in `Smothering Mother magazine and throws away her pacifier. However, it soon becomes a case of better the devil you know, when Maggie chooses an incessantly squeaky toy dog as a substitute.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Stop or My Dog Will Shoot
programme.desc: Its time to join the harvest festival celebrations for the Simpson family, but when Homer discovers this doesnt involve alcohol, he tries to escape and ends up stranded in a maze of corn. Santas Little Helper gives Lassie a run for her money and saves Homer from the maze, landing the mutt a job on the Springfield police force.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Portrait of a Lackey on Fire
programme.desc: Smithers finds true love with a famous fashion designer, but his new relationship may destroy Springfield.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart feels sorry for Mrs Krabapple after Skinner stands her up again to attend to his mothers whims, so Lisa suggests he should nominate her for Teacher of the Year. However, Skinner worries that he could lose her if she wins.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: After a life-threatening illness, Georginas back on the dating scene and looking for a rugged man. Kate seeks a Viking while Ashley wants a man who can make a deeper connection.

programme.title: The Transporter 2
programme.desc: Professional driver Frank Martin is living in Miami, where he is temporarily filling in for a friend as the chauffeur for a government narcotics control policy-maker and his family. The young boy in the family is targeted for kidnapping, and Frank immediately becomes involved in protecting the child and exposing the kidnappers.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When a ruthless debt collector comes to Springfield, Homer and Neds lives are sucked into the artfully violent world of prestige TV.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Things for Ned and Homer go from bad to worse in the stunning conclusion of this SIMPFLIX prestige crime thriller.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson leads people through the prickly business of choosing a partner naked. First into the studio is 21-year-old Dom, from Hampshire, whos been nominated for the show by his mum, in a desperate attempt to break his dismal dating record.

programme.title: The Entity
programme.desc: Parapsychologists investigate a womans claims that an invisible being has been molesting her. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Green Was My Valley
programme.desc: Five Oscars went to John Fords adaptation of Richard Llewellyns novel chronicling the life of a Welsh mining family. The film charts the change from a simple time among daffodils.

programme.title: The Sheriff of Fractured Jaw
programme.desc: Having inherited a failing gunsmith business, quintessential Englishman Jonathan Tibbs decides that the real market for his wares is in the Wild West. After falling foul of both cowboys and Indians, he finds himself conned into becoming the law-man in the lawless town of Fractured Jaw.

programme.title: The Last Wagon
programme.desc: Todd is an orphaned son of white settlers bought up by the Comanche. When his wife and children are killed by three brothers, he exacts appropriate revenge. Caught and charged with murder, hes sent by wagon train to face justice, but when the train is attacked by Apaches, he is the only male adult survivor and has to escort a rag-tag band of survivors to safety while the law set off in pursuit of their quarry.

programme.title: The Longest Day
programme.desc: The heroics and tragedies of the Allied invasion of Normandy, from the tense wait for the weather to break before the huge armada could set sail, to the bloody battles fought on the beaches of France.

programme.title: Knight and Day
programme.desc: Car restorer June Havens is flying home to Boston for her sisters wedding, but after bumping into the mysterious and charming Roy Miller, she unexpectedly becomes embroiled in a fight for her life. Miller is a spy on the run, and after he and Junes seemingly innocuous encounter at Wichita airport, FBI agent Fitzgerald presume the pair are in cahoots.

programme.title: Apocalypse Now
programme.desc: The definitive examination of the American experience in the Vietnam War, which centres on captain Benjamin Willard who is sent up river to find and kill insane renegade colonel Walter Kurtz.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers are back in New Orleans, where Si tries to convince Dave to enjoy jazz. They also cook some classic local dishes such as jambalaya and gumbo.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Bikers explore Lancashire, a region with some of the best gastro pubs in the land, award winning producers of fine food and enough top quality alcohol that the bikers might even have to temper their consumption.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond decides its time for out with the old and in with the new. She makes a flank steak and mouth-watering three-cheese waffle hash browns.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond makes four meals with just five ingredients. For breakfast she makes a Mexican classic of chilaquiles and a fabulously fast steak for lunch.

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes to Sicily and in the foothills of Mount Etna, he receives a masterclass in Sicilian sweets and cooks up an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. There is also some chocolaty cannoli on the menu.

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is in Tuscany tracking down the regions hearty farmhouse cooking. There he takes spag bol to the next level and recreates a mind-blowing Jewish-Italian recipe. There is also a special beef stew with pappardelle.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The East Hampton ambulance volunteers meet once a month for a cookout to feed its 50 volunteers, and Inas pitching in with the help of local chef Joe Realmuto from Townline BBQ.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: This is French food made easy. Ina is demystifying the classics, and offering tips and techniques to get French flavour fast. There are also ideas for French table settings.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers bring the old fashioned suet pudding bang up to date, face a technical challenge of choux religieuse, and finally must create three different types of perfectly puffed pastries.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its quarter-final time for the remaining five bakers, who must each tackle a challenge using non-traditional wheat flours, bake a gluten-free dacquoise, and finally create a dairy-free novelty vegetable cake.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: On this leg of his trip, James is in Bresse, home of one Frances most famous ingredients - the Bresse chicken and uses the revered fowl in his splendid chicken blanquette.

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: James is in Burgundy, a region famous for its wines and mustard fields. Re-tracing Keith Floyds visit 30 years ago, James stops at the same vineyard for a personal tour.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Bikers explore Lancashire, a region with some of the best gastro pubs in the land, award winning producers of fine food and enough top quality alcohol that the bikers might even have to temper their consumption.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond decides its time for out with the old and in with the new. She makes a flank steak and mouth-watering three-cheese waffle hash browns.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond makes four meals with just five ingredients. For breakfast she makes a Mexican classic of chilaquiles and a fabulously fast steak for lunch.

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver goes to Sicily and in the foothills of Mount Etna, he receives a masterclass in Sicilian sweets and cooks up an epic tuna, prawn and pistachio spaghetti. There is also some chocolaty cannoli on the menu.

programme.title: Jamie Cooks Italy
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is in Tuscany tracking down the regions hearty farmhouse cooking. There he takes spag bol to the next level and recreates a mind-blowing Jewish-Italian recipe. There is also a special beef stew with pappardelle.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The East Hampton ambulance volunteers meet once a month for a cookout to feed its 50 volunteers, and Inas pitching in with the help of local chef Joe Realmuto from Townline BBQ.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: This is French food made easy. Ina is demystifying the classics, and offering tips and techniques to get French flavour fast. There are also ideas for French table settings.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers bring the old fashioned suet pudding bang up to date, face a technical challenge of choux religieuse, and finally must create three different types of perfectly puffed pastries.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its quarter-final time for the remaining five bakers, who must each tackle a challenge using non-traditional wheat flours, bake a gluten-free dacquoise, and finally create a dairy-free novelty vegetable cake.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb journeys across America to tackle daunting food challenges and sample iconic regional dishes. Enormous food options create problems for even the most voracious of eaters.

programme.title: Chef Grudge Match
programme.desc: Laila welcomes four feuding chefs to settle their grudges. Adam Sobel seeks to humiliate Matt Jennings for accidentally ruining Adams dish at an event, and Marjorie Meek-Bradley calls out Mei Lin for allowing her pet pooch to befoul her chef coat.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: Si and Dave visit the epicentre of Cajun culture, experiencing great food and great people in Lafayette. They tuck in to crayfish and groove at a jam session.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers are back in New Orleans, where Si tries to convince Dave to enjoy jazz. They also cook some classic local dishes such as jambalaya and gumbo.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers take the country roads to Nashville, for some down-home food and music. They cook biscuits `n gravy and visit the Grand Ole Opry stage.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Pep Rally, Birthday Treat and Fancy Feet
programme.desc: Buddy gets into the school spirit with a cake for Sofias school pep rally. Grace takes a special consultation for a 40th birthday cake.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Saudi Arabia And Lobster Tail Feast
programme.desc: Carlos team takes a cake order for delivery to all the way to Saudi Arabia. Back home a fight is brewing over the bakerys signature pastry.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri dives into delicious dishes on both coasts. In Palo Alto, a funky joint puts out fresh California cooking, like spicy pork and knockout gnocchi. In Wilmington, a bakery-restaurant makes major meatloaf.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Loading up the meat with bomb beef and bird; in North Conway, N.H., a funky joint is putting Thai in their tacos, and a local gem in Emerald Isle, N.C., is firin up big barbecue; in Taos, N.M., a traditional spot serves up New Mexican specialities.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri hunts down all kinds of meaty favourites, starting with an Irish pubs cottage pie in Louisville. Then he grabs Asian-barbecue fusion and burgers with toppings like pimento cheese at two stops in North Carolina.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri road trips across America to check out classic greasy spoons. With a new generation of owners and delicious homemade food, these are the kind of places that make America special.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its competition time and battle of the taste buds as Ina and her friends Eli Zabar and David Ziff plan a pot luck dinner. They each take a course with a Mediterranean theme. But first theres breakfast at Inas to discuss the plan over delicious tri-berry pancakes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Tarragon potato salad; chunky blue cheese dressing; vegetable coleslaw; guacamole.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas making over classic dishes and turning up the volume with new ingredients and cooking methods. By daring to be different Ina transforms dishes with a big flavour payoff. Theres Easy parmesan risotto, deconstructed strawberry shortcakes and a new take on breakfast, Eggs Columbian, a favourite at local cafe and speciality food store Marys Marvellous.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: This is all about comfort food as Ina cooks up modern day comfort classics. Helping her is New York caterer Mary Giuliani, famous for her miniature grilled cheese sandwich canapes plus theres French toast bread pudding and Seafood pot pie. This is comfort for breakfast, dinner and entertaining.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina surprises Jeffrey with a Birthday at a surprise location, historic building Home Sweet Home.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is working on an article about brunch and calls in the help of team brunch. Local florist Michael Grimm creates a flower display to go on the brunch table set and photographed by Miguel Florres. Theres baked blintzes with blueberry sauce, apricot butter, roasted hazelnut granola and a fruit platter.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver creates deliciously gnarly recipes for under a quid a portion, from simple stuffed chicken, microwave chilli con carne and a pizza with no oven, to turning a humble pancake into a showstopper.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes on three comfort food classics. Hes also joined by friend Christian, aka `DJ BBQ, to reveal his ultimate burger - including what cut of meat to use (chuck steak), how to cook it and what to have on the side.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is joined by his friend Gennaro to make the perfect bolognese ravioli. Jamie also creates the ultimate cheese toastie and he cooks his take on the nations favourite dish - chicken tikka masala - cooked over an open fire.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Galton Blackiston, his pal and a top-class, Michelin-starred chef. They don diving suits to swim with seals, and hire an unusual mode of transport to take them around the islands. Galton visits a sea salt farm and delivers some back to James, who uses it to cook up some wonderful salt-baked sea bass. James finds an unusual restaurant situated in the middle of a post office, and finishes off the day cooking roast duck by a stunning seashore.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to one of his favourite places in the UK: the beautiful county of Cornwall. Joining him is his close pal and Michelin-starred chef Galton Blackiston. During their trip they discover some world-class Cornish ice cream, and Galton visits Newlyn fish market to pick up some Cornish cod. James cooks the cod along with Cornish oysters and a warm tartare sauce. They both round off the day with a painting challenge on Sennen beach, where they try out their oil painting skills.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Bikers take everyday ingredients and transform them into comforting dishes that are both satisfying and surprising. Bananas are transformed into a sophisticated tarte tatin, and some simple ricotta cheese is the basis for gnudi - light, delicious Italian dumplings.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si and Dave cook dishes to feed a multitude, just the thing to impress your guests or to fill up the family. Their salmon pie with spinach and hollandaise is a showstopper, and their chocolate and cherry buns makes a perfect centrepiece.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si and Dave cook dishes for when the temperature drops and the weather calls for sugar and spice and good hearty cooking. This means oxtail soup with herby dumplings, a vegetarian chestnut and mushroom pie and a new take on an old favourite, treacle sponge.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Comfort Food
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers cook comfort food oozing with traditional values. Theres a twist on a classic pudding, a perfect teatime treat and Si and Dave take two breakfast favourites to make one lip-smacking new dish.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richmans culinary quest has him hitting the streets to seek out the best eats. First, he heads south to Tampa Bay, where he samples some authentic Mexican tacos.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richmans eating adventure has brought him to St Paul, Minnesota. First, Adam heads to Punch Pizza where hell get to taste an authentic margherita pizza pie.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: Adam ticks Yorkshire Pudding in Yorkshire off his foodie bucket list. Then, he tries Yorkshire parkin, local rabbit, and a Yorkshire tea-inspired dessert.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Britain
programme.desc: Adam heads to Kent to try a very special sandwich in Sandwich. There, he also tries Whitstable oysters, traditional fish and chips, and a new baked sensation, the cruffin.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris getting into next-level seafood, sammies and more! A joint in Baton Rouge, LA, is knocking out a killer Cuban, a Gainesville, FL, bakery is rocking an artichoke croissant, and a spot in Tallahassee, FL, is putting pineapples in spotlight.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Fieris grabbing unique eats across the South! An uncle-nephew duo is changing the game in Gainesville, FL, with a fried chicken sandwich. Plus, theres Mexican gone vegan in Dallas, and a spot in Baton Rouge, LA, is putting their stamp on pot pie.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris heading east to get a taste of the South. A smokehouse in New Hampshire is putting their own spin on meatloaf, a funky North Carolina joint is spicin up their shrimp and a historic Florida spot is making a standout seafood stew.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Loading up the meat with bomb beef and bird; in North Conway, N.H., a funky joint is putting Thai in their tacos, and a local gem in Emerald Isle, N.C., is firin up big barbecue; in Taos, N.M., a traditional spot serves up New Mexican specialities.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri road trips across America to check out classic greasy spoons. With a new generation of owners and delicious homemade food, these are the kind of places that make America special.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is travelling the country and digging into awesome bird and beef. In Longmont, a fried chicken emporium serves up their specialty, along with unique chicharrones.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs into hearty handfuls of major flavour, like next-level pizza in Tennessee, tasty tacos in Alberta, and elevated empanadas in Georgia.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a diner with fresh French bread in Kentucky, a burger spot in Minnesota, and a cafe run by an ex-marine in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. In California Guy tries a Tuscan-style Italian restaurant, in North Carolina he has fish tacos and in Honolulu its shepherds pie.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a farmers market in South Carolina, an Italian spot in New York, and finds a fresh spin on a fish classic in Honolulu.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy follows the regulars to an El Salvadoran cafe in Santa Fe, a barbecue haunt in Salt Lake City and a creole spot in Mississippi.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mediterranean spot in Tennessee, a cafe in Minnesota with wild rice French toast, and has turkey in Kentucky.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The banquet is finally here. Its the culmination of months of hard work as the winning chefs strive to plate up perfect dishes for the ladies of the Womens Institute, at Londons historic Drapers Hall where pioneers of the WI once gathered.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their summery starters. A dish celebrating childhood summer memories takes on a complicated courgette dish and a technically tricky take on the cucumber sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more intense as all the chefs take risks with their dishes. Mike Reid, Executive Chef at M Restaurants in London, shocks with when he pairs his fish with some highly unusual flavours.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a smoke filled kitchen as the three chefs prepare their summer themed mains in the hope of impressing the veteran judge. Selin Kiazim is in her element, cooking a feast inspired by the summer barbecues she enjoyed with her family.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are feeling the pressure as they attempt their technical summer desserts. With only the two highest scorers going through to tomorrows regional final, who will be sent home?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges: nd for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs have been challenged to create outstanding dishes that capture a `taste of summer. Their menus must reflect the tastes, smells and colours of everybodys favourite time of year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with a seafood specialist in the kitchen the competition is stepping up a gear. Tom is hoping hell be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon related recipe, who will come out on top? Andy Clatworthy is using his passion for historical cooking to reinvent a dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are honouring Wimbledon and the British summer with their technically challenging strawberry desserts. Only the two highest scorers will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs compete for a place in the national finals. To get there theyll have to impress the formidable panel of judges: Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mothers favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul cant afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Forbidden Knowledge: Forgotten Cities Lost in Time
Quest for the Lost Tribes
programme.desc: Filmmaker Simcha Jacobovic searches the globe for answers and investigates the lost tribes of Israel. From 12 mentioned in the Bible only Judah and Benjamin remain after they survived the Assyrian purge of nearly three millennia ago.

programme.title: The Relics of Egypt: Exploring the Largest Museum in the World
programme.desc: A stones throw from the iconic Cheops pyramids, another famous creation rises, towering over the Giza plateau: the Grand Egyptian Museum. The new museum houses thousands of archaeological artefacts, including the funerary mask of Tutankhamun.

programme.title: Operation Carpetbagger: The Secret Mission to End World War II
programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: The ongoing battles in Manchuria, China, Abyssinia, and Spain each serve as a terrible precedent in their own way.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: As the war consumes economic, agricultural and industrial resources, the extent of the war is realised across Europe, Asia and the Pacific.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how people and resources became absorbed in the war efforts by occupying forces during WWII.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: The cloak of war played a role in the genocide of WWII, allowing unfathomable horrors against innocent women, children, and men. Even within Allied nations, individuals were seen as a potential enemies and denied liberty.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores the aftermath of WWII in the wake of destruction and liberation, with tens of millions dead and millions more displaced.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies
programme.desc: In the Second World War, a new generation of planes and daredevil pilots take aerial combat to deadly new heights.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives
programme.desc: The Nazi regime lasted from 1933 to 1945 and was one of the most horrific periods of history. However, Adolf Hitler was not working alone. He had a circle of some of the most barbaric men alongside him, who helped make the atrocities possible.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: What persuaded one-time "Man of the Year" Pierre Laval to send Jews to the death camps?

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Uncovers the motives behind the people and organisations who made the choice to aid the Nazi war effort.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Viktor Arajs formed a Latvian commando unit that hunted Jews.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: If Leon Degrelle, a writer and journalist, had lived in another era, then he could have been a hero and a leader. However, he became a supporter of Hitler and ended up betraying his faith, his country and his people.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Croat Dinko Sakic ran an extermination camp in Axis-occupied Yugoslavia.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: The story of Vidkun Quisling, who declared himself Fuhrer of occupied Norway.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.
