programme.title: My Best Friends An Animal
programme.desc: Roam across America with a road-tripping chicken and meet a mouse family living in an English garden. Beat arachnophobia with a spiders help, trek with the guardian of Earths last two northern white rhinos, and celebrate more interspecies pals.

programme.title: My Best Friends An Animal
programme.desc: Learn how cockatoos and platypuses inspired new careers, then explore the shared trauma that bonded a human and an elephant for life. Get playful with 35-year-old George the turkey vulture and say a moving farewell to human-raised kangaroos.

programme.title: A Bees Diary
programme.desc: Bees are one of the most important species on the planet. Follow the trials and tribulations of two particular honeybees over two years from birth to death.

programme.title: Wild Tales From The Farm
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and life on the farm is bursting out everywhere, as twin lambs Larry and Flora leap for joy in the meadow with the other newborns.

programme.title: Arabian Seas
programme.desc: The seas of Arabia are alive with carnivores. Sharks and rays patrol the coral reefs hunting down their prey. Toxic fish disguise themselves to ambush the unwary and bizarre armoured predators devour the vulnerable. These are the Carnivores of Arabias Coral Gardens.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: There are clashes over precious resources when the dry season brings a band of gladiators to an African savannah watering hole. There is a lion-elephant territory brawl, an ant raid on a termite mound and a honey badgers dance with a deadly snake.

programme.title: Nature
programme.desc: Capturing the overlooked life of the elephant and the irreplaceable role he plays in the survival of his species.

programme.title: Colombias Wild Coast
programme.desc: Explore Colombias wild coast, a natural fortress surrounded by shark-infested seas, blanketed in impenetrable jungle and teaming with deadly snakes.

programme.title: Colombias Wild Coast
programme.desc: A young female black crab is about to undertake the biggest mission of her life - to escort her 80,000 eggs from her mountain top home.

programme.title: Volcanic Odysseys
programme.desc: Volcanic folklore is reflected in Mexican history. One of their ancient legends tells the story of the Aztec warrior Popocatepetl - a giant active volcano - who erupts to wake his sleeping princess Iztaccíhuatl, a neighbouring extinct ice capped volcano.

programme.title: Oceans Greatest Feast
programme.desc: Get a front-row seat to the planets biggest biomass migration, as billions of sardines migrate along South Africas east coast, each hoping to escape the jaws of hungry predators.

programme.title: The Bachelor King
programme.desc: David Attenborough takes a birds-eye view in this spectacular documentary, charting the king penguins attempts to survive, find love and raise a family in the unforgiving Antarctic.

programme.title: Micro Monsters With David Attenborough
programme.desc: Its a bug-eating world out there, and the documentary continues with a look at how the various critters disable the defences of their respective prey.

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: Feel the heat when the pride tries to hunt down adequate food as drought conditions push them (and their prey) dangerously close to death.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: Welcome to the shores Canadas Hudson Bay, where a unique community polar bears are battling to survive in a rapidly changing landscape.

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: From the dry plains of the interior come the most recognizable images of Australia, the monolithic red rock of Uluru, the languid, bounding Red Kangaroo. The Arid Heart of Australia is vast and iconic.

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: Created by titanic forces, the Great Dividing Range separates a thin ribbon of green from the continents arid heart.

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: Feel the heat when the pride tries to hunt down adequate food as drought conditions push them (and their prey) dangerously close to death.

programme.title: Predators

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kante and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: Ancient Enemies: In Africa, lions and wolves lead their ecosystems. Discover the strength, social strategies and hierarchies behind how they raise and protect their young.

programme.desc: See the Armstrong Siddeley, Morris Oxford, Dennis and Bedford Fire engines, speedway and much more as we look at how high-tenacity rayon cords enables tyres to withstand the stresses of a modern car.

programme.title: Out of Town
programme.desc: Jack Hargreaves recounts a trip to Shetland in search of large fish. In his second film, he witnesses pigs dealing with a bumper crop of acorns.

programme.title: South of the Rio Grande
programme.desc: The Cisco Kid and Pancho catch a district official shooting Mexican ranchers.

programme.title: Mary Had a Little Lamb
programme.desc: A charming puppet animation film about road safety.

programme.title: The Black Arrow
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Holy Finger
programme.desc: An anonymous figure protects the weak and the poor against the greedy land barons who are fighting for power and influence throughout the 15th-century Wars of the Roses in England.

programme.title: Legend of the Hidden City
programme.desc: Ram is banished to the outlands by the king after losing a battle with Dean.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sir Lancelot
programme.desc: Squire Brian is on an errand, delivering a crate of pigeons to a neighbouring castle when he runs into a band of thieves who have stolen the Ruby of Radnor.

programme.title: Batman
programme.desc: Penguin schemes to marry Barbara, but the Commissioners daughter turns out to be a third secret crime-fighter called Batgirl - and only Alfred knows.

programme.title: The Roy Rogers Show
programme.desc: Pat is kidnapped by an outlaw gang, one of whom may be in line for a million dollar inheritance.

programme.title: Captain Scarlet and the Mysterons
programme.desc: After a failed break-in at a nuclear power plant, a manhunt begins for former Spectrum officer Captain Black.

programme.title: The Adventures of William Tell
programme.desc: Tell comes face to face with his exact double - an Austrian recuited by Gessler to impersonate the resistance leader.

programme.title: The Buccaneers
programme.desc: Captain Clip West gets captured by Tempest, but manages to take over Tempests ship after escaping from his clutches.

programme.title: The Adventures of Robin Hood
programme.desc: Robin (Richard Greene) and his men come to the aid of a young lame heir (Ian Whittaker) who fears a ghost is trying to kill him. With Barbara Mullen, Charles Stapley.

programme.title: Planet Outlaws
programme.desc: A 20th Century pilot named Buck Rogers and his young friend Buddy Wade awake from 500 years in suspended animation to find that the world has been taken over by the outlaw army of Killer Kane.

programme.title: North West Frontier
programme.desc: In 1905, a rebellion on Indias north west frontier threatens the life of a young Hindu prince. Captain Scott, a British officer, rescues the boy and sets out with a small group on an old locomotive, making a perilous journey through rebel territory, but Scott and his passengers are faced not just with the marauding tribesmen, but also a traitor in their midst.

programme.title: Whiplash
programme.desc: An attractive actress is kidnapped by Mike Upton. Cobb tries to rescue her until they run into some angry gold miners.

programme.title: Hobsons Choice
programme.desc: A crusty old bootmaker attempts to retain his cheap source of labour by preventing his three daughters from marrying.

programme.title: Look at Life
programme.desc: An exciting look at some of the girls of the WRAF who trekked into the jungle, and who also take part in a jungle survival exercise, parachute jumps, and sub-aqua diving.

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: Parisian detective chief superintendent Maigret investigates murder cases.

programme.title: The Duchess of Duke Street
programme.desc: The very first episode of the classic period drama series, which is ideal for fans of `Upstairs, Downstairs. Look out for a young June `Dot Cotton Brown.

programme.title: Robinson Crusoe
programme.desc: Daniel Defoes British hero is shipwrecked on a tropical island alone, and with his man Friday.

programme.title: The Blue Lagoon
programme.desc: Two castaway children who survive a shipwreck grow to adolescence on a remote South Pacific island and experience the pangs of first love.

programme.title: Eurotrash
programme.desc: Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. Jean Paul interviews John Waters, Victoria Abril, and Germanys biggest pop phenomenon, the Irish-American Kelly Family.

programme.title: Warm Nights and Hot Pleasures
programme.desc: Three friends want to become stars, they ensure endless frauds and casting couches which put a strain on their bond.

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Chronicling the history of movie horror from the earliest experimental chillers through the unforgettable Golden Age of movie monsters.

programme.title: Shop: Orbitrek MX

programme.title: 60s Non-Stop
programme.desc: Featuring a compilation of non-stop music from the 1960s.

programme.title: Donald Campbell Mystery
programme.desc: Donald Campbell chased a 300-mph record on Coniston Water in an ageing boat and tight finances. Narrated by Robert Powell.

programme.title: The Age of Steam
programme.desc: The 1930s were the years of the depression and had a marked effect on Britains railways. However the renewal of locomotives was urgent so there was considerable change in the steam locomotive fleets.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict.

programme.title: Hunting Nazi Treasure
programme.desc: The team investigates the systematic looting of Paris during four years of Nazi occupation and tracks missing treasures to Berlin and Switzerland.

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: A rushed investigation into the death of Hitlers niece Geli Raubal ruled that she had committed suicide. However, it is not known if she actually died at her own hands, or was she a tragic victim of Hitlers overbearing affections.

programme.title: Hitlers Countdown to War
programme.desc: By September 1938, Hitler had occupied Austria and was intent on invading Czechoslovakia. But the British and the French had made it clear that they would go to war against Germany if he did so.

programme.title: The Dambusters Great Escape: Secret History
programme.desc: Christened `The Beast by Churchill, Tirpitz was Nazi Germanys most powerful and dangerous battleship. Protected by an elite squadron of German fighters, it was seen as invincible.

programme.title: Anzacs in the Face of War
programme.desc: An historical examination of the Australian and New Zealand armed forces, as they prepared for World War I and World War II. Plus details of their shared experiences and strong bonds.

programme.title: Football Heroes
programme.desc: David Beckham has had a football career that most players would be jealous of. In this 2009 retrospective we look back at his career.

programme.title: Weapons of World War II
programme.desc: The series on World War II weaponry continues with a look at the development of rockets, especially the German V1 and V2 which terrorised south-east England in 1944.

programme.title: Where Were You?
programme.desc: Four days before Christmas in 1988, Pan Am flight 103 fell in pieces from the night sky, plummeting to the ground in the small Scottish town of Lockerbie where things would never be the same again.

programme.title: World War I: The War to End All Wars
programme.sub-title: A War of Chemicals and Engineering
programme.desc: Strategies, tactics, weaponry and medical practises must evolve due to a new kind of warfare employing everything from deadly gas to tanks.

programme.title: Historys Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The B-24 Liberator more than proved its worth over Europe and the Pacific, patterning the B17 to deliver a blow to the axis powers in WWII, however it wasnt simply a bomb delivery system.

programme.title: Greatest Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The legend of Barefoot, Babe Ruths called shot, and the mysterious death of Edgar Allan Poe.

programme.title: Poldark

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon intervenes when he learns that Penny is thinking about breaking up with Leonard, but he is hampered by his inability to keep a secret. Meanwhile, in space, Howard is being picked on by the other astronauts.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is jealous when Sheldon hires an attractive female assistant named Alex. However, it turns out that Alex is more interested in Leonard, which makes Penny feel threatened. Meanwhile, Howard discovers that his return from space has been delayed by a week and the pressure begins to take its toll on his mind.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Wolowitz is disappointed when he doesnt receive a heros welcome on his return from space. Bernadette is out of action with a heavy cold, his mother is occupied with her dentist, Koothrappali has a new best friend, and Sheldon, Amy, Leonard and Penny are deeply involved in an ultra-competitive games night. Will Wolowitz ever get the recognition he deserves as an astronaut?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang celebrates Halloween with a fancy dress party at Stuarts comic book shop, but Sheldon and Amy cant agree about which costumes to wear. Leonard and Penny get a new spark in their relationship when she visits him at his laboratory. Howard just cant stop talking about his trip to space, much to the irritation of his friends, and it takes a word from astronaut Buzz Aldrin to get his feet back on the ground.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon gets excited when Stephen Hawking agrees to play against him in an online game of `Words with Friends. However, when Sheldon starts to dominate the game, Hawking is less than pleased. Elsewhere, Penny signs up for a class at community college but feels reluctant to tell Leonard about it.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey kicks smoking with the help of weekly psychotherapy sessions, realising that her nicotine addiction was due to her husband Darryl, but can Darryl trust her with the handsome shrink Gerald?

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon is approaching her 30th birthday but is unable to see a bright future ahead of her, merely one which matches up to her past. Tracey would like to throw a party for her, but has problems with the guest list.

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Darryl has been taken to hospital with appendicitis and plans to escape and spend the night with his wife. Meanwhile, Sharon sees this as an opportunity to do some entertaining of her own.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Eric has a scheme to sell his motorcycle, and Lovejoy is plagued by swindlers.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A woman and an Arab youth both covet a Berber rug for wildly different reasons.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lovejoy hopes to prevent a developer from destroying an ancient Roman mosaic.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown suspects an ulterior motive when his old adversary Flambeau is incarcerated for murder.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: When the gun used to murder a talented young boxer 15 years ago surfaces in an armed robbery, UCOS are drawn into the darker side of professional boxing in a case that causes conflict and friction among the team.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: When the team reinvestigate a 30-year-old safety deposit robbery, they find themselves unwittingly drawn into a case involving high-level police corruption that threatens the future of UCOS.

programme.title: Jonathan Creek
programme.desc: Audrey Panguitch, an old lady with only a goldfish for company, has been having strange dreams. In one of them she predicts the murder of a prominent Swiss businessman, Andre Masson, down to the last detail. When Jonathan encounters Audreys niece at a party, hes intrigued by the mystery. As Audreys predictions become ever more bizarre, Jonathan goes downmarket to prove that dreams can form in the most unexpected ways.

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: The Queen of Poisons: Part One
programme.desc: When the Mayor dies in the middle of a meeting, Judith, Becks and Suzie uncover lies, scandal and blackmail. Guest starring Peter Davison.

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: The Queen of Poisons: Part Two
programme.desc: As the suspects secrets stack up, Judith, Becks and Suzie still cant find anyone with a motive to hurt the Mayor. Guest starring Peter Davison.

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Mike and Beth take a long road trip to obtain divorce papers from his ex-wife, and Kristin and Daniel contend with the mysterious death of a man in a hotel room.

programme.title: Whitechapel
programme.desc: The drama series concludes with the second in a two-part story. With the body count rising, Miles and Chandler clash over the direction of the investigation. Having already survived the killers wrath once, Morgan Lamb is of particular interest to the team - especially Chandler. As the chase escalates, will the detectives be able to put their differences aside in the face of their toughest adversary yet?

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Havers is spending her compensation money learning Aikido at a retreat in Cornwall. Lynley is also in Cornwall visiting his mother, when a horse trainer who lives on a neighbouring estate is found hanged in his stable. It seems like suicide, but when Lynley discovers a connection to a local smuggling racket he becomes suspicious.

programme.desc: Boycie and Marlene drive Tyler back to his old university where his band, `Puddle of Agony, is about to perform its very first gig. programme.title: Wagspiracy: Vardy v Rooney
programme.desc: In 2019, Wagatha Christie gripped Britain as Coleen Rooney accused Rebekah Vardy of leaking stories about her to the tabloid press. Despite huge media attention on this social media scandal, and a trial that ended up in the high-court in the summer of 2022, with Rooney emerging victorious, the inside story of how and why this extraordinary moment in British culture came to pass, has never fully been told - until now.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage Site. Paul joins the crowds and finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose packs his waterproofs and heads for Derwentwater and Borrowdale. Borrowdale is officially the wettest place in England, and he meets those who have battled against flooding and have learned to live with extreme weather events. Along the way, he tells the story of Millican Dalton, the Professor of Adventure, who ran his own expeditions in the Lake District more than 70 years ago. Paul also heads for the summit of Great Gable, one of the Lakes best known peaks.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Paul Rose has as adrenaline overdose on his way to Coniston, beginning with entering a retro cycling event on a penny farthing before joining the mountain bikers of Grizedale Forest. Paul also relives the final moments of Donald Campbell in his attempt to break the world water speed record on Coniston Water, and ends his trek with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the areas best known hills: The Old Man of Coniston.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the final destination in explorer Paul Roses trek through the Lake District. This corner of the western Lakes is off the normal tourist track but it boasts Englands highest mountain. Before heading for the tough Lakeland summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the countrys most scenic railways, he has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek travels to the Yorkshire coast to pick up a 1963 Austin J2 Paralanian campervan, described as the Rolls Royce of campervans.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Sunderland to pick up a real Italian beauty - a 1972 Lancia Fulvia Zagato. Red and racy, the car has seen better days, but Dereks still impressed.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Machine Gun
programme.desc: No matter how many soldiers you have, how smart your generals are or how daring your strategy is, a war will not be won if there is not enough bullets, planes or food.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: As the Cold War escalates, most Nazi hunting is abandoned. Suddenly, Nazis are able to return to normal life or find refuge overseas. Thousands begin new lives in South America.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Ham House in the London borough of Richmond-upon-Thames. Asian Art specialist Lee Young examines an exquisitely decorated 18th-century Chinese robe that was once kept in a dressing-up box.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow is at Dyffryn Gardens near Cardiff, where treasures include a necklace made from some of the largest pieces of Baltic amber that jewellery specialist John Benjamin has ever seen. Jon Baddeley admires a humble compass once owned by local man Edgar Evans, who was part of Scotts ill-fated Antarctic expedition and one of the first of the party to lose his life on the ice. Frances Christie admires a vibrant flower painting by artist Bryan Organ.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a Mark 2 Jag and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a present for his wife.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team have a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck.

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels through the Congo, the second-greatest rainforest on earth. In one of the toughest journeys hes ever undertaken, Simon travels five hundred miles across dense tropical jungle.

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: More British sauce and French farce in the long-running sitcom. Theres something to celebrate at Renes cafe. Its his birthday and he gets a series of surprises, not all pleasant.

programme.title: Black Panthers of WW2
programme.desc: The story of the history-making 761st tank battalion composed entirely of African-American servicemen. During WW2 they were the first US armoured unit of its kind to enter combat. Along with fighting abroad they also had to fight racism at home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael takes a pot shot at the sport of kings at a country estate, where the king dallied with his mistress. He learns the ropes aboard an Edwardian wherry on the Norfolk Broads and joins variety performers to tap dance on Cromer Pier. In Cambridge, he investigates the student days of the young Prince of Wales and the novelist, E. M. Forster, author of `Howards End.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill
programme.desc: Michael joins a garden party where a fourth generation citizen of Letchworth introduces him the citys community spirit. Heading into the capital, Michael discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII and samples the monarchs favourite tipple, the Kings Ginger, invented to keep him warm in his horseless carriage.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his Edwardian Bradshaws guide, Michael Portillo arrives in West Croydon, where he uncovers a once-celebrated, now forgotten,mixed-race composer with an uncannily familiar name. With the modern British rail network now half the size of the Edwardian one, Michael is delighted to discover a railway renaissance in Three Bridges.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Crossing the Solent with a ticket to Ryde, Michael boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton. His destination is Osborne House, where the future King Edward VII spent much of his childhood.

programme.title: How To Build A Motor Car
programme.desc: A visit behind the scenes with the small team in Dartford who hand-build the iconic open-top two-seater Caterham 7 sports car. See exactly how the Caterham 7 is built up from raw materials like aluminium and fibreglass.

programme.title: How To Build A Motor Car
programme.desc: A look at how Liverpool supercar manufacturer Briggs Automotive Company make their £270k BAC Mono R: the worlds first single-seat, road-legal supercar. With only 40 of the vehicles ever to be made in the world, this is precision engineering.

programme.title: How To Build A Motor Car
programme.desc: We follow the 35-hour build of a new Land Rover Defender, where over 3300 individual parts and 70 years of innovation come together to create a vehicle for any terrain: river or mountain, jungle or desert.

programme.title: How To Build A Motor Car
programme.desc: Factory 56, a brand new facility in Germany, opens its doors as we follow the ultimate configuration of a £120,000 Mercedes-Benz S-Class, from the chassis build to the main assembly floor.

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle wants Rene to set up a mobile radio station to broadcast propaganda.

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: (A Woman Never Lies) Gorden Kaye, Carmen Silvera, Vicki Michelle, Michael Percival, Hilary Minster. Farcical wartime sitcom co-written by David Croft of Dads Army fame. Theyre still searching for that fallen Madonna with the big..... so Herr Flick gives Helga a truth drug! (Subtitled)

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: More British sauce and French farce in the long-running sitcom. Theres something to celebrate at Renes cafe. Its his birthday and he gets a series of surprises, not all pleasant.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie mends a leaky pump on the Naughty Lass on the Grand Union Canal. In Watford, he visits a TV legends home and buys gas from a friendly fuel boat.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock.

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom tries out water dowsing so he can fill his new pond. He and engineer friend Sam Lovegrove fix some church bells and then put a surf lifesaving clubs quad bike back on the beach.

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on another epic journey around the world, uncovering extraordinary stories from the southern hemisphere. The first leg sees Simon crossing the beautiful landscapes of Namibia and Botswana, having a close encounter with hungry cheetahs, visiting the worlds largest diamond mine and making a gruelling crossing of the Kalahari Desert in the search of the famous San Bushmen.

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: This leg of the journey crosses South Africa, Mozambique and the beautiful Island of Madagascar. Simon comes face to face with the dramatic Zimbabwean refugee crisis, meets giant rats helping to clear land mines in Mozambique, eats soup made from the penis of the zebu (a type of ox), and visits a wild town where thousands of poor Madagascans are digging for sapphires.

programme.title: Inside Bentley: A Great British Motor Car
programme.desc: Bentley Motors have been building bespoke cars for the rich and famous for almost a century, and for the first time in their long history, the doors of its historic home in northwest England have been opened to the cameras.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family business starts a new era as Charlie heads off on his first solo collection, to pick up an Austin van from the North York Moors.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team, including Andy Joynson, buy the right kind of Land Rover, but its got the wrong engine. Can they clean up its emissions with a modern motor?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A collection of Austin Mini Metros arrive for auction. Also going under the hammer is a baby Fiat 850 coupe and a haulier offloads part of his vintage commercial vehicles collection.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Theres an unlikely arrival at the North Yorkshire car auction - an Argentinian Army Mercedes G wagon that saw action in the Falklands War.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has three scooters to collect, while also finding room for a Daimler and a teardrop caravan.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek makes his way to Blackpool to pick up what will be the highest priced item at the next auction. But, the 1970 Aston Martin DBS needs a tow to the local garage.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Over the years Derek has learnt to always expect the unexpected. So when Call the Midwife star, Cliff Parisi, drops off his Bedford CA campervan its just another day at the office.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores the Edwardian era and the golden age of detective fiction between the wars - the time of Dr Crippen, Agatha Christie and the films of Alfred Hitchcock.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage Site. Paul joins the crowds and finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose packs his waterproofs and heads for Derwentwater and Borrowdale. Borrowdale is officially the wettest place in England, and he meets those who have battled against flooding and have learned to live with extreme weather events. Along the way, he tells the story of Millican Dalton, the Professor of Adventure, who ran his own expeditions in the Lake District more than 70 years ago. Paul also heads for the summit of Great Gable, one of the Lakes best known peaks.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Paul Rose has as adrenaline overdose on his way to Coniston, beginning with entering a retro cycling event on a penny farthing before joining the mountain bikers of Grizedale Forest. Paul also relives the final moments of Donald Campbell in his attempt to break the world water speed record on Coniston Water, and ends his trek with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the areas best known hills: The Old Man of Coniston.

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
